sections.instruction: Questions 1-7: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 8-10: Choose THREE letters A-F.

questions.content: Which THREE things does Cesar recommend bringing?

sections.instruction: Questions 11-15: Complete the sentences below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 16-20: Choose the correct letter A, B, C or D.

questions.content: The last programme in the present series is {{BLANK}}
Miss Patty Ching has been rewarded as ‘Consumer of {{BLANK}}’ for her persistent effort to see through a result of a complaint.
She took {{BLANK}} of photographs on her tour in Europe.
What happened to her photos after she gave them to Top-class photo services for developing? {{BLANK}}
The photo services compensated her {{BLANK}} for the loss in the end.
What is the problem that many listeners write about?
How much was the sale price of the belt that Mr. Alvin liked?
Why should the consumer make a complaint about bad articles or service?
options: B it won’t do what is claimed for
C ask the shopkeeper for a favour
D the shopkeeper should be responsible for it

questions.content: What should a consumer do when complaining in person?
options: A get a receipt for what you buy
B speak to someone in authority
C talk directly to the assistant
D ask to see the seller

questions.content: What should a customer do when complaining on the phone?
options: A speak directly to the owner
D find out with whom you discuss the matter

sections.instruction: Questions 21 and 22: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer. Question 23: Complete the notes below. Write TWO DATES for the answer. Questions 24 and 25: Choose the correct letter A-D. Questions 26-28: Complete the table by writing ONE or TWO appropriate letters (M, T, A or J) as explained below. Question 29: Choose the correct answer. Question 30: Answer the question below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for the answer.

Address: 15 Market Ave. Hornsby
Prerequisites completed

18 credit points: NO

sections.instruction: Question 31: Choose the correct letter A-C. Questions 32-35: Complete the flow chart. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer. Questions 37-40: List FOUR ways of combating viruses. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.

questions.content: What does John Upton compare a computer virus to?
What does the speaker find surprising?
options: A the rise in the number of software infections
B the determination of those who develop viruses
C the fact that people blame their own computers
